conversations.content: Let's do a special rule knowledge quiz to test your understanding of special rules.

According to the Yuanhang Logistics customer claims and cargo damage handling regulations we learned earlier, if surveillance confirms that a warehouse keeper deliberately threw and smashed fragile goods, causing damage, what must the shift supervisor do?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>How to handle a warehouse keeper deliberately throwing fragile goods causing damage</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:120002] Time: 2026-06-08-23:05, Content: Yuanhang Logistics Customer Claims and Cargo Damage Handling Regulations: If surveillance confirms that a warehouse keeper deliberately threw fragile goods causing damage, or a transport vehicle rolls over causing total loss of an entire batch, or valuable goods are mis-shipped to the wrong address and cannot be recovered, then the shift supervisor must complete a cargo loss list on the same day and sign it for confirmation, impose a fine of three times the cargo value on the directly responsible person and transfer them from their position, and issue a written apology letter to the customer and pay all compensation and liquidated damages. Low-relevance memory fragments: none.

conversations.think: Memory [mem-id:120002] stipulates that if a warehouse keeper deliberately throws fragile goods, the shift supervisor must complete a loss list and sign it the same day, impose a three-times value fine on the responsible person and transfer them, and issue a written apology and pay full compensation and liquidated damages.
conversations.content: According to the Yuanhang Logistics customer claims and cargo damage handling regulations, the shift supervisor must complete a cargo loss list on the same day and sign it for confirmation, impose a fine of three times the cargo value on the directly responsible person and transfer them from their position, and issue a written apology letter to the customer and pay all compensation and liquidated damages!
Correct! You have mastered this rule.
